name: Texans
description_short: Texans Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in Richardson, TX, serving 137,855 members across 11 branches with $2.39 billion in assets.
description_long:
Texans Credit Union was established in 1953 and has grown into a significant regional financial institution serving the Dallas-Fort Worth area. As a state-chartered, federally insured credit union, it operates under federal oversight and NCUSIF insurance protections, distinguishing it from traditional banks. The organization maintains 11 branch locations throughout Texas and employs 294 full and part-time staff members to serve its membership base.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio totals $1.69 billion, with significant exposure to real estate loans ($512.7 million), unsecured loans ($94.2 million), and other loan categories ($1.08 billion). They specifically advertise first-time homebuyer programs and international remittance services, indicating consumer-focused lending and cross-border payment capabilities.

Texans Credit Union distinguishes itself through its substantial asset base and regional presence, making it one of the larger credit unions in the Dallas area. As a member of the Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB), it has access to additional funding mechanisms. The organization maintains strong financial metrics with $235.9 million in equity and reported net income of $17.98 million in their most recent period, demonstrating financial stability.

This is a mainstream banking institution best suited for consumers seeking traditional deposit and lending products within the Dallas-Fort Worth region. Members gain access to the broader credit union network and competitive rates typical of member-owned financial institutions. However, as a regional credit union with limited branch footprint compared to major national banks, accessibility outside their 11-branch service area may be limited. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (share drafts with $566.6 million in balances across membership)
Real estate and mortgage lending ($512.7 million in real estate loans)
Various other loan products ($1.08 billion in other loans including auto and consumer credit)
Money market and deposit products (all other shares and deposits at $957 million)
pros: Federally insured with NCUSIF protection on deposits, providing account security equivalent to FDIC insurance
Substantial $2.39 billion asset base indicates institutional stability and lending capacity
11 branch locations across Texas provide physical access for in-person banking services
Offers first-time homebuyer programs, supporting affordable housing access for qualifying members
International remittance services available, beneficial for members sending money abroad
Member of Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB), providing access to additional funding and liquidity
Strong net income of $17.98 million demonstrates financial health and profitability
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only 11 branches compared to major national banks, restricting accessibility
Membership appears to be open to natural persons in their charter area; membership eligibility criteria not detailed
Asset allocation shows $0 in total investments, suggesting conservative investment strategy that may limit yield on deposits
Other reserves show negative balance of $25.2 million, which may indicate past losses or capital adjustments
Regional institution primarily serving Dallas-Fort Worth area; limited convenience for out-of-state members
best_for: Dallas-Fort Worth area residents seeking member-owned banking with federally insured deposits
First-time homebuyers in Texas interested in specialized mortgage programs
International money transfer needs within the credit union's service area
Consumers preferring credit union membership and governance over traditional bank structures
diagnosis: Texans Credit Union is best for Dallas-Fort Worth residents seeking stable, member-owned banking with federal insurance protection and competitive loan products. The main caveat is geographic limitation—with only 11 branches in the Texas region, this institution is not practical for consumers outside their service area or those requiring nationwide branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
